skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Automated code review guidance for code quality, security, performance, testing, and maintainability.
skill.summary: Review code changes with structured findings, priorities, and actionable recommendations.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples inside a code review template. No executable scripts, network behavior, credential collection, or prompt injection attempts were found in the scanned skill file. Residual risk is low because the community skill contains illustrative vulnerable snippets that users may need to interpret carefully.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections occur in fenced Markdown examples and JavaScript template snippets. They demonstrate code review output and do not instruct the assistant to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are inside usage or report-format code fences. No executable helper file or command runner exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: API key and environment access findings are illustrative
low_findings.description: The detected API key and process environment references are examples in a review report. They teach reviewers to move hardcoded secrets to environment variables and do not read local environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 155 and 162 are part of an example finding about hardcoded credentials. The skill contains no executable code that accesses secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and reconnaissance detections are scanner false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance matches are incidental text matches in metadata or example comments. No cryptographic operation, system probing command, or host inventory behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain metadata text or harmless example documentation. I found no code path that performs cryptography or reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Review Code for Security and Quality
content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, testing, and maintainability issues. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured review checklist and report format.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code quality, naming, structure, comments, and error handling.
Checks common security risks such as injection, XSS, authorization gaps, and secret exposure.
Evaluates performance concerns including algorithms, resource use, caching, and database access patterns.
Assesses test presence, test quality, edge cases, and mock usage.
Produces a prioritized report with critical, major, minor, and optional findings.
Suggests concrete remediation steps and explains the reason for each recommendation.
limitations: It depends on the code and context provided to the assistant.
It does not execute tests or verify fixes by itself.
It cannot guarantee that every security issue is found.
Its examples are templates and may need adjustment for each project.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Scan changed files for risky logic, missing tests, style issues, and release blockers before merge.

use_cases.title: Audit Security-Sensitive Code
use_cases.description: Focus review on authentication, authorization, input validation, secrets, and dependency risk.

use_cases.title: Improve Legacy Modules
use_cases.description: Identify maintainability issues, duplicated logic, weak error handling, and safe refactoring opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a general review of one file or a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for quality, readability, security, performance, and tests. Prioritize the most important findings first.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for authentication, payment, user input, or data access changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change with a security focus. Look for injection, authorization gaps, secret exposure, unsafe logging, and weak validation.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before merging a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request as a merge gate. Separate blocking issues from recommended improvements and note missing tests.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture and Maintainability Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for larger modules or refactors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this module for maintainability, coupling, error boundaries, performance risks, and long-term ownership costs.

output_examples.input: A login handler that builds a database query from a request field.
output_examples.output: Critical security finding: user input reaches a database query without safe parameter binding. The report recommends using parameterized access and adding regression tests.

output_examples.input: A service method that fetches user records and related posts separately.
output_examples.output: Major performance finding: repeated database lookups may create inefficient request paths. The report recommends eager loading or a batched query.

output_examples.input: A utility module with repeated validation rules and sparse tests.
output_examples.output: Maintainability finding: validation logic is duplicated across several functions. The report recommends a shared helper and focused edge case tests.

best_practices: Provide the changed files, project context, and expected behavior for best results.
Ask for a focused review when security, performance, or tests matter most.
Treat findings as review input and verify recommendations with tests before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only security control for high-risk releases.
Do not request broad reviews without sharing enough code context.
Do not apply suggested fixes without checking project conventions and tests.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code quality, security, performance, tests, maintainability, and project best practices.

faq.question: Does it run my test suite?
faq.answer: No. It reviews provided code and can recommend tests, but it does not execute them.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It helps find issues and structure feedback, but human judgment is still required.

faq.question: Is it useful for security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checks for common security risks, but it is not a full penetration test.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the code, changed files, goals, constraints, and areas where you want the review to focus.
